findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new signature at `AmbientIsotopyClassProd.lean:41`, while the existing unary continuous `map` is already arbitrary (`AmbientIsotopyClass.lean:111`, `F : C(X, Y) → C(W, Z)`) and the smooth binary analogue is arbitrary in its quotient codomain (`SmoothEmbedding/AmbientIsotopyClass.lean:196-205`).
findings.fix: Give `map₂` the natural signature with extra variables `{W Z : Type*} [TopologicalSpace W] [TopologicalSpace Z]`, `F : C(X, Y) → C(X', Y') → C(W, Z)`, and result `AmbientIsotopyClass W Z`; then define `prodMap` as the product specialization.
findings.issue: `AmbientIsotopyClass.map₂` is under-generalized: it only descends operations landing in `C(X × X', Y × Y')`, even though the construction works for any target continuous-map type.

summary: The new product operation is fine, but the public continuous `map₂` combinator is introduced at a product-specific type instead of the natural quotient-map level.
